confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_fe6ba6d8-1043-47ef-adff-865e12ea9be1 read back as received

payload.authority_note: Operator explicitly asked to submit ergonomics, CAPA, and doctrinal proposals after the architect-loop issue.

incident.correction: Architect reviewed and closed completed tasks, amended task 46 Required Work into an actionable handoff, nudged Bob, and committed/pushed governance state.
incident.root_cause: Architect had not completed the review/closure duty before interpreting Bob no-work as an actual empty queue. Builder correctly could not self-review or close because Bob is builder, not reviewer/admin.
incident.what_happened: Bob reported no work available while tasks 42, 44, 45, 49, 50, 51, and 54 were completed but awaiting review/closure. Task 46 became visible only after task 45 review/closure, and then immediately blocked on placeholder Required Work.

observations.finding: No-work for Builder can mean pending Architect work, not system idle
observations.impact: Operator may think Builder is stuck or useless when the next required action belongs to Architect/reviewer authority.

observations.finding: Pending reviews are a blocker class and should be surfaced as such
observations.impact: The system should say which role must act next and which task will unblock after review closure.

observations.finding: Architect loop needs a canonical next command or checklist
observations.impact: Without a disciplined loop, Architect may nudge Builder repeatedly instead of closing reviews, amending handoffs, or routing blockers.

requests.text: Add a CAPA/doctrinal rule: when Builder reports no work, Architect must check pending reviews, blocked tasks, and underspecified handoffs before treating the queue as empty.
Enhance work-next/workboard output to report blocker owner explicitly, e.g. blocked_by_review_owner=architect/operator and unblocks_task=46 after task 45 closure.
Provide a compact architect-loop command that processes or lists: pending reviews, in-progress architect-owned tasks, underspecified handoffs, blocked builder tasks, and next nudge target.
Document that Builders finish/report; Architects/reviewers close and unblock. A Builder no-work report is evidence for Architect review, not necessarily evidence of no system work.

payload.summary: Builder reported no work while several tasks were completed and awaiting architect/operator review. The apparent no-work state was caused by pending reviews and underspecified follow-up tasks, not lack of useful work. Narada should make review closure and blocker-owner surfacing first-class in the architect loop.
payload.title: Make architect review closure an explicit duty-loop gate
